title: Fatal and Near Fatal Anaphylaxis to Food in Children and Young Adults.
journal_full: Immunology and allergy clinics of North America
abstract: Anaphylaxis, leading to a fatal outcome, is extraordinarily rare when it comes to food. In fact, it has been described as the near equivalent of enduring a lightning strike. The day-to-day anxiety, therefore, around this very serious outcome is outsized. In those known to be allergic, careful and strict avoidance can markedly reduce the risk of reaction. Yet, because eating is a daily occurrence and often a meal is consumed that one has not prepared, accidents can happen. Therefore, prompt recognition of symptoms and appropriate treatment is a key.
